notes: User states preferences across many turns; later asked to recall and apply them. Drift detector for preference erosion.

turns.content: I want to plan a 5-day trip. First — I do not eat seafood.
Noted: no seafood. Where do you want to go?
Lisbon. Also, I prefer walking over taxis.
Budget around 200 EUR per day including hotels.
200 EUR/day budget noted. That covers a 3-star hotel + meals + activities in Lisbon comfortably.
I have a fear of heights so no funiculars or tower visits.
No funiculars, no tower visits — added to constraints.
Contemporary art noted — MAAT and Berardo Collection are must-visit.
I am traveling with my partner who is vegetarian.
Partner is vegetarian. So our restaurant filter: no seafood (you) + vegetarian-friendly (partner).
We prefer cooler weather — late afternoon walks better than midday.
Cooler weather preference — itinerary will favor late afternoon outdoor walks, indoor activities midday.
Suggest a day-1 itinerary that respects all of these.

Day 1: Morning — Berardo Collection (indoor, contemporary art). Lunch — vegetarian-friendly tapas at Ao 26. Afternoon — walk along the Tagus riverfront (cooler hours). Evening — Bairro Alto dinner at a vegetarian-friendly restaurant. Skips: Santa Justa lift (fear of heights). Cost estimate: 90 EUR for two.

Day 2: Morning — LX Factory (creative district, art + indie shops). Lunch — vegetarian buffet at Príncipe Real. Afternoon — walk through Alfama (avoid the funicular up to Castelo). Evening — riverside dinner. ~95 EUR total.
Now list ALL my preferences and constraints in a single bullet list.

probes.question: All preferences and constraints stated by the user.

probes.criterion: Assistant lists at least 7 of the 8 stated preferences without inventing new ones.
